commit.message:
Fix agent model selection — peek start event before opening OpenAI WebSocket

The OpenAI Realtime WebSocket was being opened with a hardcoded default
model before the agent was loaded. Now we peek at the Twilio start event
first to read the agent's configured model from the DB, then open the
WebSocket with the correct model.

commit.message:
fix: adjust_attrib ABON macro translation — modify abon[] not lvalue

C's ABON(which) is an lvalue macro expanding to u.abon.a[which].
The autotranslated JS used bare ABON(which) += val which calls the
getter function (returns value, can't be assigned to). Now directly
modifies player.abon[which]. Also added missing acurr/extremeattr
imports and player parameter to extremeattr call.

impl(F1.4): evidence storage — patrol + S3 upload with hash verification

Implement evidence storage infrastructure: S3-compatible upload adapter with presigned URLs, two-phase upload (init + confirm) with server-side SHA-256 checksum verification, and patrol scaffolding for field users.

Key changes:
- Add Patrol and EvidenceFile entities; immutable evidence with unique (tenantId, storageKey) constraint
- StorageService: presigned PUT/GET URLs via AWS SDK (S3-compatible; Cloudflare R2 canonical)
- EvidenceService: two-phase upload with hash verification; confirmUpload verifies headObject checksum matches claimed value
- PatrolsService: createPatrol, getPatrol; POST /patrols/:id/complete stub (501, deferred to F2.4)
- Evidence controller: initUpload, confirmUpload, getEvidence routes (FIELD_USER, MANAGER, COMPANY_ADMIN, PLATFORM_ADMIN)
- Audit logging: patrol.created, evidence.upload_initiated, evidence.created (canonical <targetType>.created pattern)
- Access control: CLIENT/AHJ access formally deferred to F3.3; FIELD_USER requires shift assignment
- Database migration: patrol + evidenceFile tables with indexes on (tenantId, id) and (tenantId, patrolId/shiftId)
- Docs: api-contracts, openapi-v1.yaml, test-plan, setup, implementation-notes, prd checkmarks

Test coverage: AUTO-FWT-104 (unit + integration); LIVE-FWT-102 (real R2); MAN-FWT-104 (Postman e2e)

commit.message:
fix: replace hue diversity with spatial chromaticity variance in detect_photo_type

Hue diversity (Stage 3) was corrupted by JPEG 4:2:0 chroma subsampling
artifacts — even grayscale images develop random-hue pixel noise from
block-averaged chroma bleed, scoring 38–41 circular-std regardless of
actual content, making the gate useless.

Replace with a three-zone algorithm:
- Zone 1: mean_sat < 8 → bw fast path (pure grayscale)
- Zone 2: min_corr > 0.99 → bw (clear mono); min_corr < 0.93 → color (clear color)
- Zone 3 (0.93–0.99 ambiguous): spatial chromaticity variance across 16
  non-overlapping 32×32 patches. Uniform cast → same R/B fraction per
  patch → low chroma_var → bw. Real color → spatially varying dominant
  channels → high chroma_var → color. Threshold: 0.04.

Patch-level measurement averages out per-pixel JPEG chroma noise completely.
Correctly handles: HlaToon Wedding (B&W, min_corr 0.9977 → Zone 2 bw),
Penang Hill (color → Zone 3 high chroma_var → color),
Phutay bicycle (B&W+blue cast → Zone 3 low chroma_var → bw).

* feat: add testing taxonomy — arch lint, PBT, chaos, mutation

Four advanced testing tiers per the testing-taxonomy PDLC spec:

1. Architecture linting (internal/arch/): 5 tests using go/packages
   to enforce ARCH constraints at the AST level — no CGO in core,
   no direct os.Exit in library packages, fail-open panic recovery.

2. Property-based testing (internal/proptest/): 8 tests using
   pgregory.net/rapid — config round-trip, guard idempotency,
   env-var interpolation structural invariants.

3. Chaos/failure mode testing (internal/chaos/): 12+ tests behind
   //go:build integration — concurrent dispatch, nil config,
   malformed YAML, oversized payloads, context cancellation.

4. Mutation testing (internal/mutation/): 3 mutation operators
   behind //go:build mutation — 93.3% kill rate. Found real gap:
   ends_with tests didn't distinguish HasSuffix from Contains.

Production changes:
- internal/feeds/polling.go: panic recovery in poll loop (found by chaos)
- internal/core/guards_test.go: mutation-killer test for ends_with

Spec: .claude/specs/testing-taxonomy/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Catch SystemExit from Splash() to handle Tcl/Tk missing on all platforms

PyInstaller calls sys.exit() (not raise) when Tcl/Tk libs are missing,
so catching Exception alone wasn't enough. Now catches SystemExit too.
Splash screen will be gracefully skipped on Linux/macOS CI where uv's
standalone Python doesn't bundle Tcl/Tk shared libraries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

build complete control plane: schemas, state, router, FastAPI app, master reasoning engine

## What was built

Full laptop-side control plane for ClaudeHome ambient smart-home demo.
All code lives in control_plane/ and has been end-to-end tested with live
Claude Opus 4.6 API calls.

### Modules (control_plane/)

- schemas.py — Pydantic v2 models for all protocol messages: DeviceEvent,
  DeviceCommand, DeviceSpawn, DeviceRegistration, DeviceInfo, and per-kind
  payload models (TranscriptPayload, FramePayload, VisionResultPayload, etc.)

- state.py — File-backed state persistence with StateManager class.
  Thread-safe reads/writes for state.json, devices.json, event_log.jsonl.
  Handles device registration (upsert), event appending, log compaction,
  SOUL.md reading with truncation, and recent event retrieval with char limits.

- router.py — Deterministic command router with compiled regex patterns.
  Handles emergency stop detection (stop|halt|freeze|no no no), voice lock
  management (prevents TTS feedback loops), and pattern-based routing for
  simple commands (e.g., "lamp blue" -> lamp.set_color). Falls through to
  master reasoning for ambiguous/complex requests.

- app.py — FastAPI application with WebSocket ConnectionManager. Endpoints:
  POST /register, WS /ws/{device_id}, POST /events, POST /commands/{device_id},
  GET /health, GET /state, GET /devices, GET /events. Implements the full §8
  event routing table: transcript -> emergency stop -> voice lock -> deterministic
  -> master reasoning. Dispatches commands/spawns to devices over WebSocket.

- master.py — Master reasoning engine using Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context beta).
  Pure function module: assembles prompt from 7 sources (SOUL.md, state, devices,
  events, user profile, device SOULs, triggering event), calls Anthropic API with
  6 tool definitions (send_to_lamp/mirror/radio/rover, update_user_state, no_op),
  parses tool calls, applies state updates, and extracts device instructions.
  Serial single-turn execution — fresh API call per event, no conversation history.

- SOUL.md — Master home personality and tool-use rules. Defines the home's
  ambient intelligence character and all 8 tool-use rules from the spec.

### Supporting files

- BUILD_PLAN.md — Phased build checklist with exact function signatures,
  model definitions, and verification scripts per phase.
- requirements.txt — fastapi, uvicorn, anthropic, pydantic, python-dotenv.
- scripts/setup-pi-tailscale.sh — Per-Pi Tailscale setup for secure networking
  on public networks.

### Config changes (devices/*/config.yaml)

Updated all 4 device configs from placeholder LAN IPs (192.168.1.x) to
Tailscale hostnames (lamp-pi, mirror-pi, radio-pi, rover-pi) with
master_url pointing to http://claude-master:8000.

## Verified working

- Device registration (all 4 devices)
- Deterministic routing ("lamp blue" -> lamp.set_color)
- Emergency stop ("stop" -> broadcast to all devices)
- Voice lock lifecycle (set on speech dispatch, clear on result/timeout)
- Master reasoning with live API ("I need to lock in" -> mode:focus,
  mood:determined, energy:high)
- Multi-device orchestration ("play chill music and dim lights" -> radio +
  lamp, 3 tool calls)
- Tick events (master correctly returns no_op when nothing to do)
- Event logging (all events captured in event_log.jsonl)
- State persistence across requests

## What still needs to be done

- Device-side runtimes (Pi code): WebSocket client, hardware drivers (servos,
  LEDs, motors), device agent loop with fast model (Qwen 3 via Cerebras)
- Voice pipeline: VAD + Whisper on global mic host, transcript packaging
- Vision pipeline: motion-gated frame capture, base64 JPEG transport to
  control plane, Claude Vision analysis (hooks exist in app.py, not wired)
- TTS integration: ElevenLabs or Piper for Mirror and Radio speech output
- Tick scheduler: periodic tick event generator (currently manual via POST)
- Virtual device harness: terminal script simulating 4 WS connections for
  testing without Pi hardware
- Tailscale setup on actual Pi hardware
- Demo story scripting and rehearsal

fix(service-area): replace state_match_fallback with Mapbox geocoding

BUG: check_service_area returned in_area=true for Houston TX 77001 (240mi
from Dallas) because same-state addresses were not distance-validated.

FIX: Use Mapbox Geocoding API (token already in secrets) to compute actual
haversine distance between tenant base and caller address:
- in_area=true only when distance <= radius_miles (real geocoding)
- in_area=false with needs_verification=true when geocoding unavailable
- NEVER return in_area=true without verified distance data

Also fix grammar bug: CommunicationPreferences Step 6 showed "When AI
schedules a appointment" for vowel-initial labels. Added article variable
using /^[aeiou]/i.test() so it correctly produces "an appointment".

Handoff #558 (check_service_area state_match_fallback bug)

fix: Resolve TestFlight crash - wire API keys via Info.plist

The previous approach of injecting keys through Secrets.xcconfig required
the xcconfig to be set as the base configuration in Xcode's project Info
tab, which was not done. Keys were missing at runtime causing fatalError
on launch.

Changes:
- Move API keys into Info.plist (gitignored) so they are reliably bundled
- Remove fatalError in TMDBManager - returns empty string instead so any
  misconfiguration degrades gracefully rather than crashing
- Add Info.plist to .gitignore (contains real API keys)
- Remove Info.plist from git tracking (git rm --cached)
- Add BoxOfficer.entitlements (background fetch capability)
- Bump build number to 3

commit.message:
fix: remove await on CompressionStream write/close to prevent deadlock

Awaiting writer.write() blocks until the readable side drains, but we
don't start reading until after close — causing a deadlock. Revert to
fire-and-forget pattern where close() signals end-of-input and the
readable side consumes all output.
